summary: Quantinuum reports a major milestone in error-corrected quantum computing: using only 98 physical qubits, its team generated 48 error-corrected and 64 error-detected logical qubits—an efficiency breakthrough. Their method involves “nesting” highly efficient error-detecting codes (specifically, the iceberg code) to create logical qubits with a world-record 2:1 physical-to-logical qubit ratio. Logical qubits demonstrated error rates up to 10 to 100 times lower than physical qubits. In a notable demonstration, 64 error-detected logical qubits were used to simulate quantum magnetism at a scale difficult for classical computers. Dr. DeCross, a lead author, characterizes the code structure as “braiding together ropes made out of ropes made out of ropes.” This advance underpins future studies in quantum materials.
